last_progress_at.description:
Timestamp of the last progress-bearing event observed for this job
(distinct from `heartbeat_at`, which is just the liveness ping time).

sequence.description:
Monotonic heartbeat sequence number for this job, per
`docs/pipeline-unification/schemas/event-schema.md`'s `JobHeartbeat` shape.

LogFieldSet.description:
The canonical structured-log field set. See the "Logs" table in
`runtime/observability-contract.md` for the required-field list and the
"Forbidden in logs" list (raw auth headers, tokens/API keys/cookies, raw
env values, unredacted private prompts/responses, unredacted local
absolute paths in public logs).
LogFieldSet.title: LogFieldSet

LogFieldSet_LogLevel.description:
Log severity. Mirrors `tracing::Level`'s five levels so `LogFieldSet` can
be built from either a `tracing` event or a non-`tracing` log sink.

SourceProgressEvent_ApiError.description:
The shared, transport-neutral error shape.

Every crate reports failures as an `ApiError` so CLI, REST, MCP, jobs, logs,
and progress streams render one consistent structure.

details.description: Redacted structured context (safe key/value pairs only).

visibility.description: Where this error may be surfaced.

SourceProgressEvent_ErrorSeverity.description: How severe an error is and whether it terminates the item/job.

oneOf.description: Non-fatal issue, full behavior preserved; not terminal.
Behavior reduced but acceptable by policy; may or may not be terminal.
Required work failed; terminal for the affected item/job.

SourceProgressEvent_ErrorStage.description: Pipeline/transport stage an error is attributed to.

oneOf.description: Missing fields, bad types, unsupported flags.
Internal/unclassified failure within the active operation phase.

SourceProgressEvent_ErrorVisibility.description:
Where a context entry (or error) may be surfaced.

From the "visibility" field in the error shape: `public`, `internal`,
`sensitive`.

oneOf.description: Safe to render to any caller, including remote/unauthenticated.
Internal diagnostics; render only to internal/admin surfaces.
Sensitive/secret-adjacent; never render raw, redaction required.

SpanFieldSet.description: The canonical bounded span/log field set.
SpanFieldSet.title: SpanFieldSet

StreamEvent.description:
The contracted flat SSE/MCP streaming envelope. `data` carries the
kind-specific payload (a `SourceProgressEvent` for `progress`, the
route-specific result DTO for `final`, a `{ "text": ... }` object for
`token`, etc.) — see the contract doc for the per-kind validation rules.
StreamEvent.title: StreamEvent

StreamEvent_ApiError.description:
The shared, transport-neutral error shape.

Every crate reports failures as an `ApiError` so CLI, REST, MCP, jobs, logs,
and progress streams render one consistent structure.

details.description: Redacted structured context (safe key/value pairs only).

visibility.description: Where this error may be surfaced.

StreamEvent_ErrorSeverity.description: How severe an error is and whether it terminates the item/job.

oneOf.description: Non-fatal issue, full behavior preserved; not terminal.
Behavior reduced but acceptable by policy; may or may not be terminal.
Required work failed; terminal for the affected item/job.

StreamEvent_ErrorStage.description: Pipeline/transport stage an error is attributed to.

oneOf.description: Missing fields, bad types, unsupported flags.
Internal/unclassified failure within the active operation phase.

Where a context entry (or error) may be surfaced.

From the "visibility" field in the error shape: `public`, `internal`,
`sensitive`.

oneOf.description: Safe to render to any caller, including remote/unauthenticated.
Internal diagnostics; render only to internal/admin surfaces.
Sensitive/secret-adjacent; never render raw, redaction required.

StreamEvent_StreamKind.description:
`StreamEvent.kind` — the flat set of streaming event kinds shared by SSE
and MCP streaming, per `docs/pipeline-unification/schemas/event-schema.md`
("StreamEvent Shape").
